km.Inglaterra: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton於1290年出生在英國。她與Johannes Whitton結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton于1290年出生在英国。她与Johannes Whitton结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Whitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Whitton in Morebattle (Roxburghshire). The placename is from Old English hwīt ‘white’ + tūn ‘farm, village’, as with many of the places in 2 below.

English: habitational name from any of the places called Whitton (Durham, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Northumberland, Shropshire, Suffolk) or Witton (Cheshire, Durham, Lancashire, Herefordshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Warwickshire, Worcestershire, North Yorkshire); or possibly from Wyton (Huntingdonshire, East Yorkshire); or possibly from Weeton, Market Weighton, or Little Weighton (East Yorkshire). The placenames do not all share the same etymology but most of their post-medieval spellings are interchangeable. Most of the placenames derive from the Old English personal name Hwīta or Old English hwīt ‘white’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some, for example Witton in Warwickshire, derive from Old English wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farmstead’ + tūn, and some others, for example Witton in Norfolk, derive from Old English widu ‘wood’ + tūn. Whitton in Lincolnshire derives from the Old English personal name Hwīta (genitive Hwītan) or Old English hwīt (dative hwītan) + ēg ‘island’. Witton in Lancashire derives from the Old English personal Witta + Old English tūn.
English: in Devon, this surname is a variant of   Whiddon  .

variants.name: Whitten
meanings: English: variant of   Whitton   or   Whiting  .

variants.name: Whetten
meanings: English, Scottish, or northern Irish: variant of   Whitton  .

variants.name: Whiddon
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from any of numerous minor places in Devon named Whiddon. Some are named with Old English hwīt ‘white’ + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’; others with Old English hwǣte ‘wheat’ + dūn or denu ‘valley’.

variants.name: Whiton
meanings: English (Essex): variant of   Whitton  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
History: James Whiton of Hingham, Norfolk, England, came to Plymouth, MA, in 1635.

nameMeanings.name: Johannes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton was born in 1290, in England. She married Johannes Whitton. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton est née en 1290, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé Johannes Whitton. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton wurde 1290 in England geboren. Sie hat Johannes Whitton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton è nata il nel 1290 nel England. Lei sposato Johannes Whitton. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whittonは1290年にイギリスで生まれた。Johannes Whittonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton(은)는 1290년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Johannes Whitton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton nasceu em 1290, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Johannes Whitton. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton родилась в 1290 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johannes Whitton. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Miss Johannes Whitton nació en 1290, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Johannes Whitton. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
